decks.notes: This deck focuses on aggressively disrupting the opponent's game plan while deploying powerful threats to pressure the opponent's life total. By utilizing a mix of disruptive spells, efficient creatures, and powerful planeswalkers, the deck aims to control the battlefield and force the opponent to play on the back foot. The main strategy is to disrupt the opponent's plans with cards like Consign to Memory and Prismatic Ending, while generating card advantage and tempo with cards like Teferi, Time Raveler and Quantum Riddler. The creatures in the deck, such as Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Phelia, Exuberant Shepherd, provide additional threats and ways to close out the game quickly. Overall, the deck aims to disrupt and outpace the opponent while applying pressure on multiple fronts.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on utilizing efficient creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Guide of Souls to quickly overwhelm the opponent. Supporting cards like Goblin Bombardment and Galvanic Discharge provide additional reach and removal, while Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Phlage, Titan of Fire's Fury offer powerful finishers. Ranger-Captain of Eos and Seasoned Pyromancer provide card advantage and resilience, while Arena of Glory and Thraben Charm help control the board. The deck aims to put pressure on the opponent early and maintain a steady stream of threats to secure victory.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on quick and aggressive creature plays, supported by spells that deal damage and provide utility. The goal is to overwhelm the opponent with a mix of efficient creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Guide of Souls, backed up by removal like Galvanic Discharge and Goblin Bombardment. The deck also includes cards like Seasoned Pyromancer and Ranger-Captain of Eos for card advantage and additional flexibility. Ajani, Nacatl Pariah provides a finishing touch with its powerful abilities, while cards like Voice of Victory and Thraben Charm offer additional support and protection.

The main concept of this Dimir Control deck is to utilize a mix of counterspells, removal spells, and card advantage to control the opponent's board and hand while maintaining card advantage through draw spells like Consult the Star Charts and Wan Shi Tong, Librarian. The deck also incorporates graveyard hate with cards like Cling to Dust and Orcish Bowmasters to disrupt opponent strategies that rely on the graveyard. The goal is to outvalue the opponent by efficiently controlling the game and grinding out incremental advantages until victory is achieved.

This deck focuses on utilizing aggressive creatures to quickly overwhelm the opponent. By playing efficient threats like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Territorial Kavu, combined with powerful spells like Lightning Bolt and Leyline of the Guildpact, the deck aims to apply constant pressure and close out the game as fast as possible. The deck also includes cards like Scion of Draco and Phlage, Titan of Fire's Fury to provide additional power and flexibility. By curving out with aggressive creatures and disruptive spells, this deck looks to take control of the game early and maintain its advantage until victory is achieved.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on fast and aggressive creature attacks, supported by burn spells and powerful legendary creatures like Ajani and Ragavan. The strategy is to overwhelm the opponent with early creature plays, backed up by efficient removal spells like Galvanic Discharge and Goblin Bombardment. Phlage, Titan of Fire's Fury also adds a devastating late-game threat. The deck includes some control elements like Static Prison and Thraben Charm to handle opponent threats, while also utilizing cards like Ocelot Pride and Voice of Victory for additional synergy and support.

This deck aims to disrupt opponents' strategies while applying pressure through efficient creatures and removal spells. It combines aggressive creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer with disruptive cards like Teferi, Time Raveler and Consign to Memory to control the board and prevent opponents from executing their game plan. The inclusion of powerful finishers like Phlage, Titan of Fire's Fury and Phelia, Exuberant Shepherd ensures that the deck can close out games quickly once it gains control. The mana base supports the deck's color requirements and enables it to cast its spells consistently.

This Grixis Control deck focuses on using a variety of control spells like Counterspell, Fatal Push, and Cryptic Command to disrupt opponents' strategies while generating card advantage through cards like Snapcaster Mage and Consult the Star Charts. The deck also utilizes spells like Cling to Dust and Sheoldred's Edict for graveyard interaction and removal. The deck aims to control the board and outvalue opponents with powerful card advantage engines like Snapcaster Mage and Wan Shi Tong, Librarian, ultimately leading to a decisive victory.

The deck focuses on a fast and aggressive playstyle while also disrupting the opponent's plans with taxing effects. It includes powerful creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Phelia, Exuberant Shepherd, along with removal spells like Prismatic Ending and Solitude to control the board. The deck also includes cards like Ephemerate and Fable of the Mirror-Breaker for extra value and card advantage. By combining aggression with disruptive elements, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent and secure a quick victory.

This deck aims to quickly establish a board presence through aggressive creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Phage, Titan of Fire's Fury while disrupting opponents with cards like Teferi, Time Raveler and Quantum Riddler. The deck also utilizes spells like Prismatic Ending and Consign to Memory to control the board and protect its creatures. With a mix of removal, disruption, and aggressive threats, this Death and Taxes deck seeks to overwhelm opponents and secure a swift victory.

This aggressive deck focuses on utilizing powerful creatures like Scion of Draco and Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer to apply pressure on the opponent. Leyline of the Guildpact and Leyline Binding help to control the board while Arena of Glory provides additional utility. Stubborn Denial offers protection against removal spells, while Consign to Memory provides card advantage. The deck also includes a small splash of white and blue for versatile cards like Teferi, Time Raveler and Indatha Triome. Overall, this deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of aggression and control elements.

The main concept of this Modern 1 vs 1 deck is to quickly fill the graveyard with cards like Life from the Loam, Faithless Looting, and Stinkweed Imp in order to fuel the Dredge mechanic. This allows for a combo-heavy strategy that aims to bring out powerful creatures like Arclight Phoenix while also utilizing burn spells like Lava Dart and Lightning Bolt for disruption. The deck also includes cards like Creeping Chill and Conflagrate for additional utility and finishers. The goal is to overwhelm the opponent with a flurry of graveyard interactions and fast-paced plays.

This deck focuses on assembling the Allosaurus Rider combo using cards like Neoform and Eldritch Evolution to search for key pieces. The deck also includes cards like Generous Gift and Veil of Summer to protect the combo and disrupt opponents. A key strategy is to use Atraxa, Grand Unifier and Xenagos, God of Revels to pump up Allosaurus Rider for a game-winning attack. With efficient card draw and interaction, this deck aims to quickly assemble its combo pieces and overwhelm the opponent with a powerful board presence.

The deck focuses on controlling opponents through counterspells and removal spells while disrupting their strategies with cards like Wan Shi Tong, Librarian and Undercity Sewers. It also utilizes card draw and library manipulation to maintain the advantage and out-grind opponents. The archetype of Dimir Control shines through resource management, disruption, and a calculated approach to win conditions. With a combination of control elements and efficient threats, the deck aims to dictate the pace of the game and come out on top through patient and strategic play.

This deck focuses on controlling the game through a combination of powerful creatures, disruption spells, and mana acceleration provided by the UrzaTron lands. By utilizing cards such as Thought-Knot Seer and Glaring Fleshraker to interact with the opponent's hand and battlefield, along with Karn, the Great Creator as a versatile threat, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan while setting up powerful finishers like Ulamog, the Ceaseless Hunger. The deck also utilizes Trinisphere and Chalice of the Void to slow down the opponent's spells, creating a favorable board state for the player.

The deck focuses on quickly tutoring for and playing Allosaurus Rider to generate mana, then comboing off with Eldritch Evolution or Neoform to bring out Ghalta, Stampede Tyrant or Griselbrand for added power. The deck also includes various support cards such as Disciple of Freyalise, Planar Genesis and Xenagos, God of Revels to further enhance the combo potential. Additionally, the deck includes some protection with cards like Pact of Negation and Veil of Summer to ensure the combo goes off smoothly and efficiently.
